name: pdca-skill-creator
description: Create PDCA-based Codex skills with creator-process isolation, business-facing SKILL.md output, mandatory implementation-confirmation gates, reference-based audit records, business-core-first generation, and stronger confirmation gates.

interface.longDescription: Create Codex skills that follow a PDCA loop with Plan, Do, Check, and Act stages while preserving the business core first. Work is isolated into four periods: creator evolution, business-skill generation, runtime and checking, and evidence-based review. Incoming business requests are first wrapped as skill-creation goals so creator sessions do not accidentally execute the final business task. Generated business SKILL.md files are business-facing entry documents: trigger conditions, inputs, commands, outputs, checks, boundaries, and review entry points stay there, while creator wrapping, requirement-confirmation tables, maturity audits, provenance, and gate evidence are written to references. Before executable scripts, plugin structure, scheduled entries, or smoke tests are generated, the creator must present an implementation-confirmation document covering script flow, AI decision boundaries, risks, outputs, quality gates, and continuation conditions. Period-1 business encapsulation and page exploration are isolated behind bounded subagent contracts: a business subagent returns only a proposed Do/Check contract, an exploration subagent returns only a probe summary, and the main agent reviews and converts both into skill artifacts. Neither subagent can write final outputs or enter runtime. Business data, run logs, check results, and report values can be written only by scripts or deterministic tools, never by AI prose. Each L3/L4 run emits a source-processing-result manifest with script version, hashes, record counts, evidence paths, and exit status; checks block baseline updates or external sync when provenance is incomplete. The creator also requires Do-script plans, script-design documents, AI decision checklists, implementation-confirmation records, install verification, configurable Python entrypoints, sample-based crawler checks, network diagnostics, maturity grading, contract-consistent checks, creator-leak checks, and clean plugin directories.
defaultPrompt: Use $pdca-skill-creator to create a skill for a recurring workflow.
Use $pdca-skill-creator to upgrade an existing workflow skill.
Use $pdca-skill-creator to design a self-checking inspection skill.
Use $pdca-skill-creator to run iterative creator use case tests and improve the creator rules.
